title: Quekettia peruviana
intro: Quekettia peruviana, also known as the "Quekettia orchid," is a species of flowering orchid native to the tropical forests of Peru. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its bioactive constituents such as alkaloids and phenolic compounds. The bioactive constituents of Quekettia peruviana include queckettine and peruvoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Quekettia peruviana is used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Quekettia peruviana is classified in the plant kingdom as an orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Cymbidieae. Within the Cymbidieae subfamily, Quekettia peruviana is placed in the tribe Cymbidieae tribe. The genus Quekettia is further divided into several species, with Quekettia peruviana being one of them. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
